goal: Fix two Related Links bugs on the reading result: (1) the 'Related Internal Links' heading overlapping the card link below it; (2) reversed-card thumbnails shown as a rotated-180 upright image, which looks ridiculous in the small thumbnail.
root_cause: (1) reading.js built the panel title as a <header> element; a global, over-broad `header { position: fixed }` rule in styles.css (meant for #site-header) leaked onto it, pinning the title out of normal flow so the first .related-links__hero slid up underneath (measured 16px overlap, head computed position:fixed). (2) reading.js loaded the UPRIGHT art and added .is-reversed -> CSS rotate(180deg) for reversed cards in 'flip' mode.
fix: (1) Changed the related-links title element from <header> to <div> (same .related-links__head class/CSS) so the global header rule no longer matches it -> title back in flow, 18px gap restored, no overlap (verified live by forcing position:static: gap 18px, stillOverlaps false). (2) For reversed cards in the related-links thumb, load the dedicated reversed art via buildCardImageUrls(card,'reversed') -> candidates [reversedUrl, uprightUrl, backUrl] and drop the .is-reversed rotate class; degrades to un-rotated upright then back if reversed art is missing. Both changes are in reading.js; bumped the shared cache-bust ?v=20260625a->20260625b site-wide so the versioned reading.js delivers; mirrored to www/.
verified: reading.js node --check OK; live DOM: head was position:fixed overlapping hero by 16px, and forcing it static restored an 18px gap with no overlap. Reversed-art path uses the same resolver the app already uses for card faces (backUrl always present as final fallback). Post-deploy: confirm on a reading result with a reversed related-link card.

goal: Make the WordPress widget plugin (wordpress-plugin/meowtarot-tarot-widget) pass WordPress.org plugin-directory guidelines so it can be submitted.
root_cause: Guideline 10 blocker: the plugin auto-injected a front-end attribution backlink to meowtarot.com on every render. WP.org requires any front-end credit link to be opt-in (default off). Also readme had a stale 'Tested up to: 6.5' and no external-services disclosure for the meowtarot.com iframe (Guideline 7/8).
fix: Attribution link is now OPT-IN, default OFF: new helper meowtarot_widget_attribution_enabled() reads a per-instance `attribution` attr (yes/no) else falls back to site option meowtarot_widget_show_attribution (default 0); render only emits the <a> when enabled. Added a Settings page (Settings -> MeowTarot Tarot) with a single checkbox, register_setting + sanitize-to-0/1, a 'Settings' plugins-screen link, and a block ToggleControl + boolean block attribute. readme.txt: Tested up to 6.5->7.0, Stable tag/version 1.1.0->1.2.0, added '== External services ==' iframe disclosure + opt-in attribution docs + changelog. Plugin header + MEOWTAROT_WIDGET_VERSION -> 1.2.0. Rebuilt wordpress-plugin/meowtarot-widget.zip (top-level meowtarot-tarot-widget/, 3 files). Second pass after WP.org automated Plugin Check: fixed ERROR outdated_tested_upto_header (6.8->7.0, current WP) and WARNING textdomain_mismatch by renaming the plugin 'MeowTarot — Free Tarot Widget' -> 'MeowTarot Tarot Widget' so the name-derived slug = text domain = folder = meowtarot-tarot-widget. Contributors: dunkinkr.
verified: node --check block.js OK; PHP CLI not installed locally so no php -l (manual review: balanced braces, esc_*/sanitize on all output + the option, checked() on the box). NOT yet submitted to WP.org (needs founder WP.org username for Contributors + the Chrome submission run) and NOT yet committed.

goal: Build a Chrome new-tab extension (SEO Widget Project) that shows a daily cat-tarot card, reusing the widget — distribution + brand citation + recurring habit loop feeding MRU.
fix: New chrome-extension/ (MV3): manifest.json overrides chrome_url_overrides.newtab -> newtab.html; newtab.js reuses the EXACT 78-card dataset from widget.html (extracted line 111 so it never drifts) and shows a deterministic 'card of the day' (FNV-1a hash of local date -> stable index; changes at local midnight) with a 'Draw another' random pull. Every link points to meowtarot.com with utm_source=chrome_ext (card -> /cards/<slug>/, 'Read full meaning', 'Free tarot readings at MeowTarot'). EN/TH via navigator.language. MV3 CSP: no inline script (JS in newtab.js); Google Fonts + cdn.meowtarot.com images allowed (default policy only restricts script-src). Icons 16/48/128 generated from cdn logo.jpg via ImageMagick. Store assets: 1280x800 screenshot. Packaged chrome-extension/meowtarot-newtab.zip (manifest at zip root).
verified: node --check newtab.js OK; manifest.json json-valid; headless-Chrome render at 1280x800 shows the daily card (King of Swords for 2026-06-26) with real CDN art, name, keywords, and all 3 backlinks. NOT yet published (needs the $5 Chrome dev account + the Chrome publish run).

goal: Resolve the WordPress.org plugin review round-1 security issue (unescaped sidebar widget title) so MeowTarot Tarot Widget can be re-uploaded.
root_cause: Reviewer flagged meowtarot-tarot-widget.php:195 — the classic sidebar widget echoed apply_filters('widget_title', $instance['title']) without escaping, so a filtered title could output unsafe HTML (XSS vector).
fix: Wrapped the title in esc_html( apply_filters('widget_title', ...) ); kept before_title/after_title (theme wrappers) raw with a clarifying comment + narrowed phpcs:ignore to .OutputNotEscaped. Bumped version 1.2.0->1.2.1 (header + MEOWTAROT_WIDGET_VERSION) + readme Stable tag + changelog. Rebuilt wordpress-plugin/meowtarot-widget.zip. Same fix already pushed to the public GitHub repo dunkin-novice/meowtarot-widget (cd22f73) and delivered to ~/Downloads/meowtarot-widget.zip for re-upload. The name/ownership review items need NO code change — keep name+slug, founder proves brand ownership via WP.org account email -> hello@meowtarot.com.
verified: grep confirms esc_html on the title line; Version + Stable tag = 1.2.1; zip rebuilt (manifest/readme/php at meowtarot-tarot-widget/ root). PHP CLI absent locally; manual review only.
